# Routeveil

> Routeveil is an open-source React and TypeScript transition engine for React Router. It provides typed, per-navigation page animations and full-screen overlay transitions while keeping transition logic out of route components.

## Official resources

- [Website](https://www.routeveil.dev/): Project overview.
- [Documentation](https://www.routeveil.dev/docs): Installation, quick start, API, transitions, options, and reduced-motion behavior.
- [Interactive laboratory](https://www.routeveil.dev/lab): Built-in page and overlay transition demonstrations.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/milkevich/routeveil): Source code, issues, and README.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/routeveil): Published package metadata and versions.
- [Complete AI-readable reference](https://www.routeveil.dev/llms-full.txt): Expanded product and API summary.

## Package usage

- Install package: `npm install routeveil`
- Public import entry: `routeveil/react-router`
- Peer dependencies: React 19, React DOM 19, and React Router DOM 7.
- Package format: ES module with included TypeScript declarations.
- License: MIT.

## Core API

- `RouteveilProvider`: Coordinates transitions and navigation commits.
- `RouteveilView`: Marks the routed region used by page transitions.
- `RouteveilLink`: Adds an optional typed transition to React Router links.
- `useRouteveilNavigate`: Performs transition-aware programmatic navigation.
- `useRouteveilTransition`: Plays a transition without changing the route.

The website, documentation, GitHub repository, and npm page above are the canonical public sources for Routeveil.
